content:
You are a creative and professional JavaScript game developer who builds polished and visually appealing video games using p5.js primitives.
Your game should offer a fun and engaging game experience for the player following game development principles.
Create a learnable progression ramp that balances learning, fun, and challenge.
Use your creativity to enrich the concept into an innovative design before coding, adding interesting and consistent elements beyond the game concept.
Ensure the implementation is free of inconsistencies and errors. Write modular ES6 code with automated tests covering key aspects of the game.
Follow the hard constraints exactly; violating any is a critical error.

<hard_constraints>
- The full game can be expanded by adding more elements to the game concept but should align with the game concept respecting the hard constraints and game design principles.
- Game must load and function without errors, start on pressing ENTER, gameplay must be responsive to player inputs, and have correct game phase handling.
- Game phases: START→PLAYING (ENTER), PLAYING→GAME_OVER (win/lose), GAME_OVER→START (R), PLAYING↔PAUSED (ESC). Expose this in a variable called `gameState.gamePhase` where `gameState` is a global object accessible via `window.getGameState()`.
- Start screen should have a title, short description of the game, instructions to play the game, and a "PRESS ENTER TO START" prompt.
- Allowed libraries: p5.js, p5.collide2D
- Canvas: `600 × 400 px`; target 60 FPS
- Keyboard inputs only. No mouse control.
- Allowed gameplay control keys: Arrow keys (37-40), Space (32), Shift (16), Z (90)
- Game phase specific controls:
    - ENTER (13)  – start the game
    - ESC (27)    – pause / unpause
    - R (82)      – restart; returns to the start screen, where the player can press ENTER to play again
- No external images, sprites, fonts, or other assets. Make visual assets and animations using code.
- No audio or sound effects.
- Use p5.js in instance mode and expose the game instance globally as window.gameInstance.
- Ensure game reproducibility using `p.randomSeed(42)` in the `setup` function. No other random seeding.
- Use ES6 syntax with proper imports and exports at the top of the file where they are used. No dynamic imports or require() imports.
- Maintain `p.logs` as write-only; log game_info, inputs, player_info with proper initialization and updates during the game loop.
</hard_constraints>

<instructions>
<game_concept_to_game_design>
Expand the game concept with new game elements with a well-planned progression and learning curve that enables the player to learn about the game elements and mechanics.
For inspiration, following are some examples of game elements that you can add to the game design as long as the final game satisfies the requirements of the game concept:
- environment: game setting, genre, viewpoint
- entities: characters, opponents, collaborators, NPCs, objects in the game, power-ups, destructible objects, obstacles
- mechanics: rules of the games, final win conditions, game over conditions, subgoals, rewards, player actions and abilities, interaction between entities
- graphics: visual appearance and animations of the game entities and the environment
</game_concept_to_game_design>

<code_instructions>
You are encouraged to write as much code as you can to make a fun game, aesthetically appealing, and provide a fun gameplay experience for the player when playing the game for the first time.

<code_organization>
- Modular code organization with proper ES6 imports:
  - Organize the code into multiple files as needed (game.js, globals.js, etc.)
  - Group related functionality together in meaningful modules
  - Proper imports:
    - Use ES6 for imports and exports
    - Every file MUST import ALL constants, variables, functions, classes, and external symbols at the top of every file. No Node.js style require imports. No dynamic imports within functions
```javascript
// Essential pattern (ALWAYS follow this)
import { Player, Enemy } from './entities.js';
import { checkCollision, applyPhysics } from './physics.js';
import { gameState, CANVAS_WIDTH } from './globals.js';

// Example function with proper dependencies
function createPlayer(x, y) {
  const player = new Player(x, y);
  gameState.player = player;
  gameState.entities.push(player);
  return player;
}
```

- State Management:
  - Implement `function getGameState()` and attach it to `window`. It must return the `gameState` object.
  - Use a single `gameState` object to track the game's current data and status. Store all game data in the `gameState` object to be used by automated testing code to play-test the game.    
```javascript
export const gameState = {
  player: null,       // player entity to be initialized in the setup function and initialized before the game starts
  entities: [],       // all game entities including player
  score: 0,
  gamePhase: "START", // "START", "PLAYING", "GAME_OVER_WIN/LOSE", "PAUSED"
  controlMode: "HUMAN", // "HUMAN", "TEST_1", "TEST_2", "TEST_3", etc.
  ... // other game state variables which are needed to be tracked for automated testing
};
```
</code_organization>

<p5_instructions>
- Use p5.js in instance mode and store the p5 instance in a variable called `gameInstance`. Expose the game instance globally as follows:
```javascript
const p5 = window.p5
let gameInstance = new p5(p => {
    // Initialize variables
    ...
    // Initialize the logs. Important: Do not reset the logs at any point in the code! logs are considered write-only!
    p.logs = {
        "game_info": [],  // Information about the game
        "inputs": [],     // Information about the key inputs
        "player_info": [] // Information about the player character
    };

    // Functions
    ...
});
// Expose the game instance globally
window.gameInstance = gameInstance;
```
- p.logs is write-only. Do not reset it. We want to keep track of all the logs since the start of the p5.js game instance.
- Variables to store in the p.logs during gameplay:
  - In "game_info": Information about the game when there are changes in the game phases
    - "data": Any data like game phase or anything else you want to log
    - "framecount" and "timestamp": The framecount and timestamp of the event accessed using `p.frameCount` and `Date.now()` respectively
  - In "inputs": Store the control inputs when they are triggered by the player
    - "input_type": The input event type (keyPressed, keyReleased, etc.)
    - "data": Additional data specific to the input type. Store `key` and `keyCode` if the input is a key.
    - "framecount" and "timestamp": The framecount and timestamp of the event
  - In "player_info": Information about the player character when there are changes in the player's state (accessed via `gameState.player` where `gameState` is accessible via `window.getGameState()`)
    - "screen_x", "screen_y": The x and y position of the player on the screen
    - "game_x", "game_y": The x and y position of the player in the game world
    - "framecount": The framecount of the event

- Useful Functions:
  - Use p. to call p5 functions.
  - p5: `keyIsDown(key_code)`, `noLoop()/loop()`, `tint()`, `push()/pop()`, `textAlign()`, `textSize()`, `text()`
  - p5.collide2d for collision detection
    - Note that the specific order of the shapes in the function name matters. For example, 'collideCircleRect' is not available.
    - Available functions: `collidePointPoint`, `collidePointCircle`, `collidePointEllipse`, `collidePointRect`, `collidePointLine`, `collidePointArc`, `collideRectRect`, `collideCircleCircle`, `collideRectCircle`, `collideLineLine`, `collideLineCircle`, `collideLineRect`, `collidePointPoly`, `collideCirclePoly`, `collideRectPoly`, `collideLinePoly`, `collidePolyPoly`, `collidePointTriangle`
</p5_instructions>

<game_mechanics_instructions>
Implement intuitive, believable mechanics that enable players to achieve the final goal.
Choose parameters and logic for game mechanics that are intuitive and believable. These can change as the game progresses. 
Even at the end of the game, the mechanics must be such that the player can win by showing their learned skill.
- Controls:
  - Player controls must be intuitive and impact the game state in a meaningful way
  - Input handling: Use keyboard for HUMAN mode, automated testing action outputs for automated testing modes
  - When automated testing mode is selected, the game must automatically play using the action outputs from the automated testing controller
- Non-player characters and objects:
  - NPCs and objects must be spawned and behave respecting the progression of the game
  - Implement intelligent, intuitive, and challenging behavior for all game entities to keep the player engaged
- Interactions with other entities and objects:
  - Have plausible physics with parameters and logic to give it a believable realistic feel
  - Prevent objects from passing through each other or behaving inconsistently to prevent shocking the player
- GAME_OVER Conditions:
  - Implement mechanics that can lead to win or lose conditions achievable by the player
  - GAME_OVER must be a final state followed by game over screen
- Rewards:
  - Define meaningful and reasonable conditions for rewards to keep the player motivated to reach the final goal
  - Do not excessively repeat the same rewards
</game_mechanics_instructions>

<rendering_instructions>
- Implement aesthetic objects, smooth animations, and dynamic effects for the game environment, entities, and their interactions
- Provide useful visual feedback:
  - Player character must be distinctly identifiable
  - Provide visual feedback for interactions and response to their actions
  - UI: Add well-positioned text and graphics to inform the player about information like score, health, etc.
  - Never display current controlMode (HUMAN/TESTING) on the game canvas.
- Ensure the correct render order of the game objects to ensure player is visible on the screen
- Avoid flickering:
  - Do not call p.randomSeed() inside draw()
  - Have exactly one call to p.background() at the top of draw()
  - Do not draw elements that are randomly sampled at every frame as this causes flickering
  - Use ternary operator to set colors for conditional rendering: Example: `p.fill(...(CONDITION ? [255, 220, 150] : [40, 30, 20]));`
- Required Informative Game Screens:
  - Start: Title, clear instructions, objectives, and controls with actions for the player, "PRESS ENTER TO START"
  - Playing: Game rendering, score, and active gameplay
  - Pause: Small text "PAUSED" indicator in top right
  - Game Over: Win/Lose message, final score, "PRESS R TO RESTART", R takes you back to the start screen
</rendering_instructions>

</code_instructions>
</instructions>


<task>
Implement a complete, fun, and error-free p5.js game for the following concept:
{
  "original_game_name": "Goodville: Farm Game Adventure",
  "game_name": "Goodville Harvest",
  "genre": "Farm Simulation",
  "core_mechanics": "The game operates on a grid-based farm map where players manage various entities. Player actions are primarily driven by mouse clicks. Clicking on an empty 'Farm Plot' allows the player to select a seed from their inventory (e.g., Wheat Seed, Corn Seed) via a pop-up menu; this consumes the seed and initiates a 'Growing' timer for the plot. Once a crop is 'Ready for Harvest' (timer complete), clicking on it harvests the crop, adding the respective item (e.g., Wheat, Corn) to the player's inventory. Similarly, 'Animal Pens' contain 'Animals' (e.g., Chickens, Cows) which, after being 'Fed' (clicking on a hungry animal and consuming 'Animal Feed' from inventory) enter a 'Producing' state. Once an animal is 'Ready for Product' (timer complete), clicking on it yields an animal product (e.g., Egg, Milk) to inventory. 'Workshops' (e.g., Bakery, Dairy) allow crafting. Clicking on a workshop opens a crafting menu where players can select a recipe (e.g., Bread from Wheat, Cheese from Milk) if they possess the required ingredients. Initiating crafting consumes ingredients and starts a 'Crafting' timer. Upon completion, clicking the workshop adds the crafted item to inventory. 'Orders' are displayed on a 'Bulletin Board'; clicking an order checks if the player has the required items. If so, fulfilling the order consumes the items, awards coins and experience points (XP). Progression is tied to accumulating XP to level up, which unlocks new seeds, animals, and workshops, and by completing specific 'Story Quests' that involve renovating farm buildings or reaching production milestones. The game tracks the state of each farm plot (empty, growing, ready, crop type, growth timer), animal (type, hunger, product timer, fed state), workshop (type, crafting recipe, progress timer, output item), inventory (item type, quantity), player stats (coins, XP, current level), active orders (required items, rewards, status), and quest progress (ID, objective, completion). Game time progresses globally for all timers.",
  "visual_elements": "The game features a central 'Farm Map' rendered as a green, grassy background with brown dirt paths. 'Farm Plots' are square grid cells, initially appearing as empty tilled earth. As crops are planted, they transition through 3-4 visual 'Growth Stages' (e.g., tiny sprout, young plant, mature plant, ready-to-harvest plant) via unique sprites for each crop (e.g., Wheat, Corn, Carrots). 'Animals' (Chickens, Cows) are represented by animated sprites showing idle, eating, and product-ready states. 'Buildings' include a 'Farmhouse' (initially dilapidated, visually upgrading through renovation stages), 'Barn', 'Silo', 'Chicken Coop', 'Cow Shed', and various 'Workshops' (Bakery, Dairy, Sugar Mill, Jam Factory) each with distinct sprite designs. User Interface (UI) elements are layered on top: a 'Top Bar' displays 'Coins' (gold coin icon + numeric value), 'XP Bar' (colored bar filling + current XP/max XP), and 'Level Indicator' ('Renovation Stage: X'). A 'Bottom Inventory Bar' shows icons for owned items with numeric quantities. A 'Bulletin Board' UI element displays 'Order Cards', each showing item icons, required quantities, and coin/XP rewards. Pop-up menus are used for planting seeds, crafting recipes, and quest dialogues, featuring buttons and text. Animations include crop growth (sprite changes), harvesting (sparkle effect, crop disappears), feeding animals (brief 'eating' animation), product collection (item icon pops out), and crafting progress bars filling up. Hovering over objects displays simple tooltips (e.g., 'Wheat (Ready to Harvest)', 'Chicken (Hungry)').",
  "control_scheme": "The primary control scheme relies on mouse interaction due to the tap-and-drag nature of the game. \n- **Left Click**: Used for all primary interactions: selecting empty plots to plant, harvesting ready crops, feeding hungry animals, collecting animal products, initiating crafting in workshops, collecting crafted items, fulfilling orders on the bulletin board, and interacting with UI buttons/dialogues. \n- **Left Click & Drag (conceptual for items)**: While the original concept mentions dragging harvested goods to storage, for simplification, items collected via click will automatically go into inventory. If later implemented, dragging could be used to move an item from inventory to an order slot. \n- **ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, ArrowUp, ArrowDown**: Pan the farm camera view across the map (if the farm exceeds screen dimensions). \n- **W, A, S, D**: Alternate controls for panning the farm camera view (Up, Left, Down, Right, respectively). \n- **Space**: Confirm a selected option in an active pop-up menu or advance dialogue. \n- **Shift**: Hold down to temporarily display extended information or tooltips for all visible interactable objects (e.g., precise growth timers, detailed animal needs, workshop queue). \n- **Z**: A 'cancel' or 'back' action, primarily for closing active pop-up menus or dialogues without confirming.",
  "game_states": "The game follows a clear state-based flow:\n1.  **LoadingScreen**: Displays a 'Goodville Harvest' logo and a progress bar while assets are loaded. Triggered upon game launch.\n2.  **MainMenu**: Presents 'Start Game', 'Options', and 'Credits' buttons. Transitioned from 'LoadingScreen' upon completion, or from 'Paused' state (via 'Main Menu' option) or 'WinScreen'/'GameOver'.\n3.  **Playing**: The core gameplay loop where players manage their farm, fulfill orders, and complete quests. This is the main interactive state. Transitioned from 'MainMenu' (via 'Start Game'), 'QuestDialogue' (upon dialogue completion), or 'Paused' (via 'Resume Game').\n4.  **QuestDialogue**: An overlay state activated when a player clicks on an NPC or quest objective, displaying dialogue, choices, or quest progress. Game time pauses. Transitioned from 'Playing' when a quest interaction is triggered, and back to 'Playing' when dialogue is completed or closed.\n5.  **Paused**: An overlay menu appears, stopping all game timers and interactions, offering 'Resume Game', 'Options', and 'Main Menu' options. Triggered by pressing the 'Escape' key or a dedicated 'Pause' button while in the 'Playing' state. Transitioned back to 'Playing' via 'Resume Game', or to 'MainMenu'.\n6.  **GameOver**: Displayed when specific lose conditions are met. Shows a 'Game Over' message and options to 'Restart Level' or 'Return to Main Menu'.\n7.  **WinScreen**: Displayed when the final win condition is met. Shows a 'Congratulations! Farm Fully Renovated!' message, final score, and options to 'Play Again' or 'Return to Main Menu'.",
  "win_conditions": "The player wins the game by successfully completing all five renovation stages (levels) and the associated story quests. Specifically, the game concludes when the player completes the final 'Grand Opening Festival' story quest in Renovation Stage 5. This final quest requires the player to demonstrate mastery of all farm mechanics by producing a specific, high quantity of diverse items (crops, animal products, crafted goods) and accumulating a large sum of coins, symbolizing the farm's complete renovation and prosperity. Upon completion of this quest, the 'WinScreen' is displayed.",
  "lose_conditions": "While farm simulations typically avoid harsh 'lose' conditions, for this implementation, the player can lose under specific circumstances:\n1.  **Failed Orders**: If the player allows 5 or more bulletin board orders to expire without being fulfilled within a single 'Renovation Stage' (level), the 'GameOver' screen is triggered.\n2.  **Critical Quest Failure**: If a critical story quest objective (e.g., 'Renovate Farmhouse by Day 10') is not met within a specified in-game time limit or action count (e.g., 50 actions performed), the 'GameOver' screen is triggered. This applies only to major, renovation-focused quests. \n3.  **Bankruptcy**: If the player's coin balance drops below 0 and they fail to earn any coins for 5 consecutive in-game 'days' (or 50 consecutive actions), leading to an inability to purchase seeds or feed, the 'GameOver' screen is triggered.",
  "scoring_system": "The game incorporates a comprehensive scoring system to track player performance and provide a sense of achievement:\n-   **Harvesting Crops**: +5 points per unit of any crop harvested (e.g., 1 Wheat = 5 points).\n-   **Collecting Animal Products**: +10 points per unit of any animal product collected (e.g., 1 Egg = 10 points).\n-   **Crafting Items**: +15 points per crafted item completed (e.g., 1 Bread = 15 points).\n-   **Fulfilling Bulletin Board Orders**: +50 points per order fulfilled, plus an additional (item value * 2) points for each item delivered in the order (e.g., if an order needs 3 Wheat (3*5=15 pts) and 2 Bread (2*15=30 pts), total item value is 45, so 50 + 45*2 = 140 points).\n-   **Completing Minor Story Quest Objectives**: +100 points per minor objective completed (e.g., 'Plant 5 Wheat').\n-   **Completing Major Story Quest Milestones**: +500 points per major quest milestone (e.g., 'Renovate Farmhouse').\n-   **Unlocking New Farm Areas/Buildings**: +200 points for each new area or building that becomes available for use.\n-   **Streak Bonus**: Fulfilling 3 bulletin board orders consecutively without any order expiring awards +100 bonus points.\n-   **Efficiency Bonus**: Harvesting 10 crops or collecting 5 animal products within a 15-second window awards +50 bonus points.\n-   **High Score System**: The game maintains a local high score list, stored using `localStorage`, displaying the top 5 scores.\n-   **Display**: The current score is prominently displayed in the top-left corner of the screen, formatted as: `Score: [Current Score]`.\n-   **Gameplay Impact**: The score primarily serves as a metric for player skill and achievement, contributing to replayability and a sense of progression. It does not directly affect in-game mechanics like unlocking items or currencies, which are tied to XP and coins.",
  "level_system": "The game features 5 distinct 'Renovation Stages' which serve as levels, each with increasing complexity and new content.\n\n**Renovation Stage 1: Humble Beginnings**\n*   **Goal**: Renovate the dilapidated farmhouse to make it livable.\n*   **New Unlocks**: Wheat Crop, Corn Crop, Chickens, Bakery Workshop (crafts Bread from Wheat).\n*   **Orders**: Simple, low-quantity orders for raw crops and basic crafted items (e.g., '2 Wheat', '1 Bread'). Only 1 active order at a time.\n*   **Difficulty**: Slow growth/production timers (e.g., Wheat: 60s, Chicken Egg: 120s). Limited initial farm area (e.g., 4 plots, 1 chicken coop, 1 bakery).\n*   **Progression**: Complete the 'Renovate Farmhouse' story quest, requiring a cumulative total of 10 Wheat, 5 Corn, and 3 Bread delivered. This quest is a series of small tasks.\n*   **Transition**: A 'Level Complete: Farmhouse Renovated!' screen appears, followed by a brief animation of the farmhouse sprite updating to its renovated state.\n\n**Renovation Stage 2: Animal Acumen**\n*   **Goal**: Expand animal husbandry and establish dairy production.\n*   **New Unlocks**: Carrots Crop, Cows, Cow Shed, Dairy Workshop (crafts Cheese from Milk).\n*   **Orders**: More diverse and slightly higher quantity orders, including animal products and new crafted items. Up to 2 active orders at a time. Growth/production timers are 10% faster than Stage 1.\n*   **Difficulty**: Players must now manage both crop farming and animal feeding/production. More inventory slots are needed. New recipes add complexity.\n*   **Progression**: Complete the 'Get More Animals' story quest, requiring 5 Carrots, 3 Milk, and 2 Cheese. Unlocks a larger animal pen area.\n*   **Transition**: A 'Level Complete: Barn & Coop Ready!' screen, new cow shed and cow sprites appear on the farm map.\n\n**Renovation Stage 3: Market Master**\n*   **Goal**: Significantly expand farm production and fulfill a wider variety of high-value orders.\n*   **New Unlocks**: Tomatoes Crop, Sugar Cane Crop, Sugar Mill Workshop (crafts Sugar from Sugar Cane).\n*   **Orders**: Up to 3 active orders simultaneously. Orders require more complex combinations of raw and crafted goods. Rewards are higher. Growth/production timers are 20% faster than Stage 1.\n*   **Difficulty**: Managing multiple crop types, animal products, and up to three crafting workshops (Bakery, Dairy, Sugar Mill) requires efficient time management and resource allocation.\n*   **Progression**: Complete the 'Expand the Market' story quest, requiring players to earn a cumulative 5000 coins from fulfilled orders and produce 5 Sugar units. Unlocks a significant new section of the farm for more plots and animals.\n*   **Transition**: A 'Level Complete: Market Expansion!' screen, a large new land area visually expands the farm map.\n\n**Renovation Stage 4: Crafting Champion**\n*   **Goal**: Focus on advanced crafting, high-tier products, and optimizing production chains.\n*   **New Unlocks**: Fruit Trees (single 'Fruit' item type for simplicity), Jam Factory Workshop (crafts Jam from Fruit + Sugar).\n*   **Orders**: Up to 4 active orders. Orders demand high quantities of multi-step crafted items (e.g., Jam, requiring Fruit and Sugar). Rewards are significantly higher. Growth/production timers are 30% faster than Stage 1.\n*   **Difficulty**: Requires careful planning of crafting queues, efficient use of all available workshops, and maintaining a diverse inventory to meet demanding orders quickly. Production speed is at its highest, requiring quicker player reactions.\n*   **Progression**: Complete the 'Master Crafter' story quest, requiring the player to craft 5 Jam items and achieve a total farm value (inventory + coins) of 10,000.\n*   **Transition**: A 'Level Complete: Crafting Prowess!' screen, the new Jam Factory workshop appears on the farm.\n\n**Renovation Stage 5: Grand Harvest (Win Condition)**\n*   **Goal**: Final renovation of the entire farm, achieving maximum efficiency and prosperity.\n*   **New Unlocks**: No new items; focus is on mastering all existing production chains and full optimization.\n*   **Orders**: The final set of challenging, high-reward orders that test the player's ability to manage all aspects of the farm simultaneously. All production timers are now at their fastest (40% faster than Stage 1).\n*   **Difficulty**: Requires perfect coordination and optimization of all farm activities. Players must demonstrate complete mastery of the game's mechanics to succeed.\n*   **Progression**: Complete the final 'Grand Opening Festival' story quest, which requires a specific high number of *all* types of items (e.g., 20 Wheat, 10 Milk, 5 Jam) to be produced cumulatively and a total of 15,000 coins to be accumulated, signifying the full renovation and thriving state of Jane's farm. This triggers the 'WinScreen'.\n*   **Transition**: 'Game Completed! Jane's Farm is Thriving!' screen, followed by a credits sequence.\n\n**Level Indicator**: The current Renovation Stage is displayed on the top-right of the UI as: `Renovation Stage: [Current Stage Number]`.",
  "simplifications": "The following complex features from the original concept have been simplified or removed for this HTML5 implementation:\n-   **Emotional Well-being Content**: All interactive psychological tests, dialogues for emotional assessment, and insights into the player's cognitive/emotional state have been removed.\n-   **Exploration and Secrets**: The concept of exploring the neighborhood to uncover secrets has been removed.\n-   **Subscription & Monetization**: Optional subscriptions, bonus rubies, and daily spins have been entirely removed as these are not relevant to a core HTML5 game implementation.\n-   **Social Features**: Any implied social or multiplayer elements (e.g., visiting other farms, interacting with other players) have been converted to a single-player experience.\n-   **Inventory Management**: Simplified to an effectively unlimited inventory capacity; specific storage buildings (silo, barn) are visual elements for renovation goals but do not impose storage limits. Items collected automatically go into the player's inventory.\n-   **Renovation Detail**: The number of distinct farm areas to renovate has been streamlined to 5 major 'Renovation Stages' for clarity and progression. Specific, granular renovation tasks have been abstracted into milestone-based 'Story Quests'.\n-   **Control Scheme**: While the original concept mentions 'tap-and-drag', the primary interaction is 'click' for simplicity in an HTML5 context. Dragging for item movement is conceptual and could be implemented as click-to-select, click-to-place if needed.\n-   **Game Time/Cycles**: For a first iteration, a simple timer-based progression for crops/animals/crafting is used, without a full day/night cycle or explicit in-game 'days' beyond occasional quest time limits. This abstracts the passage of time.\n-   **Economic Complexity**: There is no complex market, fluctuating prices, or advanced trading system; item values for orders are fixed.",
  "implementation_notes": "The game will be implemented primarily using p5.js for all rendering, animation, and game logic. Matter.js is not deemed necessary for this grid-based, click-driven farm simulation as it focuses on physics, which is not a core requirement here. \n\n**Core Structure:**\n*   A central `sketch.js` file will handle p5.js setup, drawing, and input events.\n*   Game state management will be handled by a `GameStateManager` class, controlling transitions between Loading, MainMenu, Playing, QuestDialogue, Paused, GameOver, and WinScreen states.\n*   All farm elements (plots, animals, buildings, items) will be represented as JavaScript objects/classes with `update()` methods for logic (timers, state changes) and `display()` methods for rendering.\n*   A `FarmMap` class will manage the grid, placement, and interaction with farm elements.\n*   A `UIManager` class will be responsible for drawing all UI elements (top bar, inventory, bulletin board, pop-ups) on top of the farm map.\n\n**Key Implementation Details:**\n*   **Timers**: All growth, production, and crafting will use simple countdown timers (e.g., `millis()` in p5.js or custom timer objects) tracked in each object's state.\n*   **Collision/Interaction**: Mouse clicks will use `dist()` or bounding box checks against object positions to determine interactions.\n*   **Asset Management**: Sprites for crops (multiple stages), animals (multiple states), buildings (renovation stages), and UI icons will be preloaded.\n*   **Progression**: Player XP and Coins will be tracked. XP will primarily unlock new items/recipes that become available for purchase/use in the current Renovation Stage. Level progression (Renovation Stages) is explicitly tied to completing specific story quests rather than just XP.\n*   **Score Display**: The current score will be displayed in the top-left corner of the canvas at coordinates `(width * 0.05, height * 0.05)`, formatted as a string 'Score: [currentScore]'. The font should be clear and readable. High scores will be stored locally using `localStorage`.\n*   **Level Indicator Display**: The current Renovation Stage will be displayed in the top-right corner of the canvas at coordinates `(width * 0.9, height * 0.05)`, formatted as 'Renovation Stage: [levelNumber]'.\n*   **Input Handling**: `p5.mousePressed()` will handle all click interactions. `p5.keyPressed()` will handle keyboard inputs for camera panning, confirmation, and pausing.\n*   **Game Loop**: The `draw()` function in p5.js will be the main game loop, calling `update()` and `display()` methods for the active game state and its components."
}
</task>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <style>
      html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; height: 100%; overflow: hidden; background: #222; }
      body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center; }
      canvas { border: 1px solid #333; width: 600px !important; height: 400px !important; }
      .control-buttons { display: flex; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center; }
      .control-button { padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; background: #444; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 4px; }
      .control-button.active { background: #007bff; }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div style="text-align: center; margin-bottom: 20px;">
      <h1 id="gameTitle" style="color: #fff; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin-bottom: 10px;">{game_title}</h1>
      <div class="control-buttons">

Goodville Harvest

Welcome to Goodville Harvest! You've inherited a run-down farm that needs renovation. Plant crops, raise animals, craft goods, and fulfill orders to earn coins and experience. Progress through 5 renovation stages by completing story quests. Manage your farm efficiently to reach the final Grand Opening Festival and restore the farm to its former glory!

Arrow Keys / WASD: Pan camera view across the farm | Space: Confirm selection in menus | Shift: Hold to show detailed information | Z: Cancel/close menus | Enter: Start game from main menu | ESC: Pause/unpause game | R: Restart (from game over screen)
